I'll pull the tower's fixed specs and stated objectives from the live case and the project docs.

You’re right to be cautious — for a trial, we should only nudge one thing at a time.

### What this tower is for

**T-100** is the atmospheric crude fractionator. Its job is to split hot crude into:

| Product | Role |
|---------|------|
| Off Gas | Overhead vapor |
| Naphtha | Overhead liquid |
| Kerosene / Diesel / AGO | Side draws (with strippers) |
| Residue | Bottoms |

The written case objective is: **match product cut targets, hold residue rate, minimize PA duty** — but the actual plant quality numbers (D86, flash, etc.) are **not filled in yet** (`target_value: null`). So right now HYSYS is converging on **flow/duty specs**, not on locked quality FINAL_TARGETs.

---

### What is Fixed / Active right now (the real numbers)

13 Active Fixed Primary specs close DOF. Reflux Ratio is Estimate-only (not Active).

| Spec | Specified (approx) | Role |
|------|--------------------|------|
| Kero_SS Prod Flow | **271.3 USGPM** | Side draw |
| Diesel_SS Prod Flow | **561.5 USGPM** | Side draw |
| AGO_SS Prod Flow | **131.3 USGPM** | Side draw |
| Naphtha Prod Rate | **670.8 USGPM** | Overhead liquid |
| Liquid Flow | **102.1 USGPM** | Overhead |
| Vap Prod Flow | **0** | Offgas ≈ none |
| PA_1 Rate / Duty | **1458 USGPM** / **−55e6 Btu/hr** | Top PA |
| PA_2 Rate / Duty | **875 USGPM** / **−35e6 Btu/hr** | Mid PA |
| PA_3 Rate / Duty | **875 USGPM** / **−35e6 Btu/hr** | Lower PA |
| Kero Reb Duty | **7.5e6 Btu/hr** | Side-stripper reboiler |
| Reflux Ratio | **1.0** (Estimate only) | Monitor — do not Activate first |

Status from the live test: **DOF = 0, converged, State E**.

---

### Aim vs what HYSYS is actually fixing

- **Plant aim (intended):** product quality / cuts (ASTM, flash, gaps) — placeholders only so far.  
- **What the solver is fixing today:** product rates + PA rates/duties + kero reb duty + zero vapor product.

So a safe trial is a **small nudge** on one Active handle (e.g. Diesel draw or one PA duty), not flipping Reflux or rewriting the whole Active set. If you want to try something, say which product or symptom you care about and we can pick the smallest one-knob move.

content.text: Before locking a build-up plan, two choices change the whole direction:

input.name: Intelligence Build-Up Direction
input.overview: A safe, layered strategy to grow CDU Assist toward (and beyond) your senior HYSYS CDU judgment—without destabilizing the hard T-100 system—by encoding your decisions as inventory-gated thin rules, not by dumping all PE docs into the engine at once.
input.plan:
# Intelligence build-up direction (discussion → strategy)

## Verdict first

**Yes, it is safe to develop intelligence now** — if you keep the rules you already wrote: thin layers, Inventory gate, interactive-only, one MV per trial, never auto-relax FINAL_TARGETs, never auto Specs.Add / structural writes.

**No, do not try to “become you” in one leap.** Two days of platform + PE shell is the right stage for **build-up**, not for autonomous senior PE. The dangerous move is stuffing all of `cdu_intel/` / `new_intelligence/` / expert modules into `column_engine.py` before quality truth and your keep/reverse judgment are live.

“Surpass you” is realistic as **consistency, memory, breadth, and speed** (same case tomorrow, every cut family, every assay variant) — not as replacing your first-principles creativity on a novel plant mess. Aim to **clone your decision process**, then **amplify** it.

---

## What you already have (do not rebuild)

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  observe[Observe COM + boards]
  state[States A-E]
  propose[Propose one MV family]
  trial[Bounded trial + snapshot]
  judge[Keep or reverse]
  memory[Trial Map trail]
  observe --> state --> propose --> trial --> judge --> memory
```

- **Platform / safety:** COM read-write, snapshot restore, DOF discipline — strong.
- **PE shell:** States, PE board, Trial Map, CDU MV preference (draw/PA/steam over RR) — good co-pilot.
- **Paper brain:** Inventory + workflow §28 + complementary D1–D6 — richer than code.
- **Gap:** judgment still mostly **solver residuals**, not **your product / cut / energy narrative**.

Canonical gate for any new rule: [`docs/INTELLIGENCE_INVENTORY_V1.md`](docs/INTELLIGENCE_INVENTORY_V1.md) (one row + one T-100 validation before code).

---

## Direction: encode *your* PE OS, not a second brain

Use this priority stack (already in project docs):

```text
L0 Refinery / case objective     ← you define in config
L1 Product FINAL_TARGETs         ← quality truth
L2 Subsystem / section           ← kero vs diesel vs PA belt
L3 Mechanism                     ← why this MV
L4 HYSYS Active GoalValue        ← only after L0–L3
L5 Bounded trial + keep/reverse  ← judge like you
```

**Build order (recommended default):**

1. **Capture your judgment** — for each live diagnose/assist, you say: symptom → mechanism → preferred MV → keep/reverse why. Log into Trial Map / short case notes. This is the training signal.
2. **Wire product truth** — fill [`config/cdu_t100_case.json`](config/cdu_t100_case.json) + enable [`config/cdu_final_targets.example.json`](config/cdu_final_targets.example.json) → `cdu_final_targets.json` with *your* plant numbers; then COM-read D86/flash/cut when ready.
3. **Quality-based keep/reverse** — Assist keeps only if product/operability improves (your narrative), not if residual score alone looks better. This is the largest single step toward “same as you.”
4. **One symptom tree at a time** — e.g. only “diesel too heavy” until it matches you on T-100; then kero flash; then PA heat balance. Do not open all §5 trees at once.
5. **Case memory (later)** — assay / slate / PA config → “what worked last time” ([`new_intelligence` D4](new_intelligence/Deliverable_4_Learning_and_Memory_Specification_v1.md)). This is how you start to *surpass* (you forget; the tool does not).
6. **Autonomy last** — Assist Loop stays interactive until trees + quality scoring pass your acceptance tests. Autonomy without your keep rule will optimize the wrong thing confidently.

**Do not prioritize yet:** structural moves, full hydraulics NLP, dumping entire expert module set into runtime, rival state machines that fight Inventory States A–F.

---

## Safety rules while intelligence grows

| Safe | Unsafe |
|------|--------|
| New rule = Inventory row + T-100 trial | Silent rewrite of Active set |
| Interactive approve each trial | Batch Assist Loop “proving” intelligence |
| FINAL_TARGET locked | Auto-relax targets to claim State E |
| One family / bounded step | Multi-knob “smart” search |
| Docs first when unsure | Code every PE bible paragraph |
| Complementary docs help Assist | Second brain that supersedes States |

T-100 is hard *because* 13 Active Fixed specs already close DOF. Intelligence should mostly **choose which existing GoalValue to nudge**, not invent a new Active philosophy every run.

---

## How to improve intelligence in practice (your role)

You are not only the user — you are the **oracle**:

1. Pick one recurring decision you make on CDUs (start: diesel off-spec, or PA duty vs draw).
2. Write 5–10 lines: observe → diagnose → MV → stop criteria → fail modes.
3. Add Inventory row → thin code path → live T-100 test with reverse ready.
4. If Assist disagrees with you, **you win** and we patch the rule — that is build-up.

Repeat until that path feels like you. Then next path. Depth before width.

---

## What “surpass” should mean here

| Dimension | Path |
|-----------|------|
| Consistency | Same PE rules every time |
| Memory | Case/assay/trial reuse |
| Breadth | Many cut symptoms coded after one is proven |
| Speed | Faster COM + PE board than manual click map |
| Creativity on novel messes | Still you + Assist; optional later learning |

---

## Near-term focus (next build slice after this discussion)

Assume we stay **interactive** and **clone your judgment first**:

- P0: Your FINAL_TARGET / quality numbers on T-100  
- P0: Keep/reverse driven by those targets  
- P1: One symptom→MV tree hardened to your standard  
- P2: Acceptance checklist (“Assist said what I would say”)  
- Defer: autonomous loop, structural escalation, full doc dump

No code changes until you confirm this direction (or redirect: general library-first, or autonomy-first).

Each new rule: Inventory row + one live T-100 validation

# Intelligence Build-Up Strategy (locked direction)

**Status:** Active — 2026-07-23  
**Product:** CDU Assist v1 — New Intelligence  
**Gate:** [`INTELLIGENCE_INVENTORY_V1.md`](INTELLIGENCE_INVENTORY_V1.md)

## Locked direction (confirm-direction)

| Choice | Decision |
|--------|----------|
| Intelligence source | **Clone PE judgment first** (oracle = you), then amplify |
| Operating mode | **Interactive-first** (`interactive_only: true`) |
| Not chosen now | Library dump into engine; autonomy-first Assist Loop |

**Surpass** means: consistency, case memory, breadth, speed — not silent redesign of T-100.

## Safety while building

- One Inventory row + one T-100 validation per new rule
- One MV family per trial; snapshot keep/reverse
- Never auto-relax FINAL_TARGETs; never auto Specs.Add; never silent structural writes
- Docs first when unsure; thin code layers only

## Reasoning stack (encode this, not a second brain)

```text
L0 Case objective (config)
L1 Product FINAL_TARGETs / quality
L2 Subsystem (kero / diesel / PA / OH)
L3 Mechanism
L4 Existing Active GoalValue nudge
L5 Bounded trial + quality-first keep/reverse
```

## First symptom tree (pick-first-symptom)

**Hardened first:** `diesel_too_heavy` — see [`symptoms/DIESEL_TOO_HEAVY.md`](symptoms/DIESEL_TOO_HEAVY.md).

Next trees only after this path matches PE acceptance on T-100: `kerosene_off_spec`, then PA heat balance.

## Near-term P0–P2

| Pri | Item | Code / config |
|-----|------|----------------|
| P0 | Plant quality / FINAL_TARGET numbers | `config/cdu_t100_case.json`, `config/cdu_final_targets.json` |

| P1 | Diesel too-heavy MV order | case `mv_preference` + expert quality routing |
| P2 | Acceptance checklist | below |

## Acceptance checklist (Assist said what I would say)

When diesel D86 95% is above target (or PE injects that symptom in tests):

1. [ ] Diagnose prefers **State C** (or quality symptom visible on PE board), not blind RR
2. [ ] First proposed MV is **Diesel_SS Prod Flow decrease** (`side_draw_rate_nudge`)
3. [ ] Second preference is **PA duty** on diesel belt (`pa_duty_nudge`), not Activate RR
4. [ ] Trial that worsens diesel quality is **REVERSED** even if residual score improved
5. [ ] Trial that improves diesel quality and stays operable is **KEPT**
6. [ ] With `interactive_only`, Assist Loop runs **one** trial then stops for PE review
7. [ ] Unavailable D86 COM does **not** fake State C hard-miss (targets stay non-gating until measured)

## Build cadence

1. PE states observe → diagnose → MV → keep why (5–10 lines)
2. Inventory row → thin code → unit test → live T-100 smoke
3. If Assist disagrees with PE → PE wins; patch rule

Defer: autonomous multi-trial loops, structural escalation, full `cdu_intel/` dump.

# Symptom tree: diesel_too_heavy (first hardened path)

**Inventory id:** `CDU-SYM-DIESEL-HEAVY`  
**Status:** CODED (routing + keep policy) — live D86 COM still PARTIAL  
**Case:** T-100 / Atmospheric Crude Tower

## PE observe

- Diesel ASTM D86 95% (or EP) **above** plant maximum
- Neighbor cuts (kero / AGO) may shift — watch gaps

## Mechanism (L3)

Too much heavy material pulled into diesel cut → reduce diesel draw first; then mid PA heat; then strip steam. Top RR is last on CDU with Active draws/PAs.

## Preferred MV order (L4) — must match `config/cdu_t100_case.json`

1. `side_draw_rate_nudge` → **Diesel_SS Prod Flow** GoalValue **down**
2. `pa_duty_nudge` → PA duty on diesel belt (prefer PA_2)
3. `side_strip_steam_nudge` → diesel stripper steam / energy
4. `reflux_or_oh_nudge` — last resort only

## Stop / fail

- Reverse if diesel quality worsens or operability fails
- Do not Activate Reflux Ratio while draws/PAs close DOF
- Do not auto-relax FINAL_TARGET

## Code hooks

| Step | Module |
|------|--------|
| Symptom classify | `cdu_quality_engine._symptom_from_reading` |
| MV seeds | `cdu_t100_knowledge.build_subsystem_routed_seeds` |
| Expert route | `cdu_expert_engine._quality_routed_hypotheses` |
| Keep/reverse | `column_engine.should_keep_trial` + `quality_trial_delta` |

def quality_targets_to_final_targets(case: CduCaseConfig) -> list:
    """Bridge configured quality targets to column_models.FinalTarget where possible."""
    from column_models import FinalTarget

    out: list[FinalTarget] = []
    for t in case.configured_targets():
        rel = "less_or_equal"
        if t.constraint == "minimum":
            rel = "greater_or_equal"
        elif t.constraint == "target":
            rel = "equal"
        out.append(
            FinalTarget(
                id=t.target_id,
                description=f"{t.product} {t.property}",
                spec_name_contains=t.property.lower(),
                component_name_contains=tuple(),
                stream=t.stream.lower(),
                relationship=rel,
                target_value=float(t.target_value),
                tolerance=t.tolerance,
                locked=True,
                hard=t.hard,
            )
        )
    return out

class ConvergenceAssistant:
    def __init__(
        self,
        columns: ColumnController,
        limits: ConvergenceLimits | None = None,
        targets: list[FinalTarget] | None = None,
        case_config: CduCaseConfig | None = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.columns = columns
        self.limits = limits or ConvergenceLimits()
        self.targets = targets if targets is not None else default_final_targets()
        self.history: list[TrialResult] = []
        self._estimates_refreshed = False
        self._flat_product_streak = 0
        self._skipped_families: set[str] = set()
        self._family_flat_counts: dict[str, int] = {}
        self._optimize_flat_streak = 0
        # Simple optimize (thin layer) — default min RR
        from column_optimize import SimpleObjective

        self.optimize_objective = SimpleObjective.MIN_REFLUX_RATIO

    def _infeasible_evidence(self) -> bool:
        if self._flat_product_streak >= self.limits.max_flat_trials_before_f:
            return True
        # Both energy and split families flat/exhausted
        if {"B_energy", "C_split"}.issubset(self._skipped_families):
            return True
        return False

    def _diagnose(self, state: ColumnState) -> Diagnosis:
        return diagnose(
            state,
            self.limits,
            self.targets,
            self.history,
            columns=self.columns,
            case_config=self.case_config,
        )

def assist(
        self,
        column_name: str,
        max_iterations: int | None = None,
        dry_run: bool = False,
    ) -> list[TrialResult]:
        """Run the controlled trial loop until State E, State F, blocked, or iteration limit.

        When case ``interactive_only`` is True (build-up default), run at most one
        trial so the PE reviews keep/reverse before further moves.
        """
        results: list[TrialResult] = []
        limit = max_iterations or self.limits.max_iterations
        if getattr(self.case_config, "interactive_only", True):
            limit = min(limit, 1)
        for _ in range(limit):
            state, diagnosis = self.diagnose_column(column_name)
            if diagnosis.engineering_state == EngineeringState.E_ACCEPTABLE:
                results.append(
                    TrialResult(
                        action=TrialAction("stop", "State E — acceptable"),
                        before_score=score_state(state),
                        after_score=score_state(state),
                        kept=True,
                        message=format_pe_board(state, diagnosis),
                        after_state=state,
                        response_class=ResponseClass.TARGET_MET,
                        pe_board=format_pe_board(state, diagnosis),
                    )
                )
                break
            if diagnosis.engineering_state == EngineeringState.F_INFEASIBLE or (
                diagnosis.recommended_strategy in {"fix_dof", "report_infeasible"}
            ):
                results.append(
                    TrialResult(
                        action=TrialAction(
                            "stop",
                            diagnosis.recommended_strategy,
                            payload={"strategy_id": "report_state_f"},
                        ),
                        before_score=score_state(state),
                        after_score=score_state(state),
                        kept=False,
                        message=format_pe_board(state, diagnosis),
                        after_state=state,
                        response_class=ResponseClass.STOP_INFEASIBLE,
                        pe_board=format_pe_board(state, diagnosis),
                    )
                )
                break
            trial = self.run_one_trial(column_name, dry_run=dry_run)
            results.append(trial)
            if dry_run:
                break
            if trial.action.kind in {"none", "manual_dof", "stop"}:
                break
            if getattr(self.case_config, "interactive_only", True):
                # PE must approve next move — do not batch Assist Loop
                break
            if not trial.kept and trial.action.kind in {
                "set_goal",
                "refresh_estimates",
                "baseline_swap",
            }:
                if len(results) >= 3 and all(not r.kept for r in results[-3:]):
                    self._flat_product_streak = max(
                        self._flat_product_streak, self.limits.max_flat_trials_before_f
                    )
                    results.append(
                        TrialResult(
                            action=TrialAction(
                                "stop",
                                "No progress",
                                payload={"strategy_id": "report_state_f"},
                            ),
                            before_score=trial.after_score,
                            after_score=trial.after_score,
                            kept=False,
                            message=(
                                "Stopped: three consecutive reversed trials "
                                "(State F evidence — not relaxing FINAL_TARGET). "
                                f"Skipped families={sorted(self._skipped_families)}"
                            ),
                            after_state=trial.after_state,
                            response_class=ResponseClass.STOP_INFEASIBLE,
                        )
                    )
                    break
        return results

"""Build-up intelligence tests — diesel tree + quality-first keep/reverse."""
from __future__ import annotations

from cdu_case_config import load_case_config
from cdu_expert_engine import build_expert_context, propose_from_expert
from cdu_quality_engine import (
    ProductQualityState,
    PropertyReading,
    QualityStatus,
    QualitySymptom,
    build_product_quality_state,
    merge_final_targets,
    quality_trial_delta,
)
from column_engine import (
    evaluate_final_targets,
    should_keep_trial,
)
from column_models import (
    ColumnSpecState,
    ColumnState,
    ConvergenceLimits,
    EngineeringState,
    FinalTarget,
)


def _t100_state() -> ColumnState:
    return ColumnState(
        name="T-100",
        degrees_of_freedom=0,
        physical_solution=True,
        appears_converged=True,
        cdu_topology=True,
        product_streams=["Naphtha", "Kerosene", "Diesel", "AGO", "Residue"],
        bottoms_molar_flow_kgmole_h=1400.0,
        specs=[
            ColumnSpecState(
                name="Diesel_SS Prod Flow",
                is_active=True,
                goal_value=0.035,
                current_value=0.035,
                error=1e-5,
                goal_display=561.5,
                display_unit="USGPM",
                mv_family="side_draw",
            ),
            ColumnSpecState(
                name="PA_2_Duty(Pa)",
                is_active=True,
                goal_value=-35000000.0,
                current_value=-35000000.0,
                error=0.0,
                mv_family="pa_duty",
            ),
            ColumnSpecState(
                name="Kero_SS Prod Flow",
                is_active=True,
                goal_value=0.017,
                current_value=0.017,
                error=1e-5,
            ),
            ColumnSpecState(
                name="Reflux Ratio",
                is_active=False,
                goal_value=1.0,
                current_value=0.7,
                error=0.3,
            ),
        ],
    )


def _reading(
    tid: str,
    *,
    value: float,
    target: float,
    status: QualityStatus,
    constraint: str = "maximum",
    hard: bool = True,
) -> PropertyReading:
    return PropertyReading(
        target_id=tid,
        product="Diesel",
        stream="Diesel",
        property="D86_95",
        value=value,
        unit="degC",
        target_value=target,
        constraint=constraint,
        status=status,
        hard=hard,
        deviation=value - target,
        read_method="test_inject",
    )


def test_case_buildup_direction_locked() -> None:
    case = load_case_config()
    assert case.interactive_only is True
    assert case.primary_symptom_tree == "diesel_too_heavy"
    assert case.mv_preference["diesel_too_heavy"][0] == "side_draw_rate_nudge"
    assert case.configured_targets()
    diesel = next(t for t in case.configured_targets() if "DIESEL" in t.target_id)
    assert diesel.target_value == 360.0


def test_final_targets_loaded_and_unavailable_not_hard_miss() -> None:
    targets = merge_final_targets()
    assert any(t.id == "DIESEL_ASTM95" for t in targets)
    status = evaluate_final_targets(_t100_state(), targets)

Yes — I get exactly what you mean.

That chain is:

**Lab assay → characterization in HYSYS → feed into the flowsheet (PreFlash first) → heater → T-100**

Lab does not give you “HYSYS-ready” hypocomponents. They give measured things like:

- Distillation (TBP / D86 / D1160 / SIMDIS — different formats)
- Density / API
- Light ends (C1–C5, sometimes H₂S, etc.)
- Often sulfur, viscosity, MW, water, salt…

You (or Oil Manager / Assay) then **convert** that into a petroleum fluid: cut points, pseudo-components, light-ends merge, property methods. Only after that is the crude stream trustworthy as feed to **PreFlash**, then the furnace, then the atmospheric tower.

That matters for intelligence too:

- If the assay conversion is wrong, **no amount of diesel-draw / PA tuning on T-100 will fix it**
- Product D86 targets we put in config are the **plant side**; the lab assay is the **feed side**
- Your case already has `PreFlash` upstream of T-100 — that’s the right mental model

So the “different format” problem is real: lab report ≠ simulation feed. Conversion is a separate PE step before column decision-making.

If you want to build that into Assist next, the natural layer is: **assay completeness / format check → characterization assumptions logged → only then allow PreFlash / column tuning**. Want to talk through how *you* usually convert lab → HYSYS (what labs send you, what you type where)?

**Separate it.** Don’t fold full lab→assay→Oil Manager conversion into CDU Assist.

### Why
CDU Assist is already a hard system: T-100, DOF, Active specs, draws/PAs, States A–F, keep/reverse. Assay work is a **different job**:

| | CDU Assist (this program) | Assay / feed program |
|--|--|--|
| Focus | Fractionator operating / convergence | Lab data → petroleum fluid |
| Objects | Column specs, Side Ops, Monitor | Assay, Oil Manager, blends |
| Failure mode | Wrong MV / DOF / quality | Wrong feed → whole case lies |
| Skill | Column PE | Characterization PE |

Mixing them in one app tends to create:

- Conflicting “brains” (tune column vs fix feed)
- Much bigger COM / UI surface
- Harder testing (“is it the assay or T-100?”)
- Scope creep that fights your thin-layer rule

Your own product line already says: **don’t stretch CDU Assist into other tower tools**. Assay conversion is even further upstream than the column.

### What *should* stay here (thin gate only)
CDU Assist should **know** feed/assay status, not **do** the conversion:

- “Assay not validated → don’t tune T-100 yet”
- Show PreFlash / feed tags from config
- Soft PE board warning: characterization incomplete

That’s a **gate**, not a second product.

### Recommended shape

```text
Assay Assist (separate)     →  characterizes crude / blend
        ↓ trusted feed stream
CDU Assist (this repo)      →  PreFlash awareness + T-100 decisions
```

Same family (“Tower Assist”), **separate program** — like you already plan Simple Column / VDU separately.

**Bottom line:** smart move is **separate program for lab→simulation conversion**; keep only a light “feed OK?” check inside CDU Assist so complexity doesn’t explode here.

print(f'{\"Name\":<28} {\"Active\":<7} {\"Est\":<5} {\"Type\":<14} {\"Goal (display)\":<22} {\"Current\":<18} {\"Err\":>12}')
print('-'*110)
for s in state.specs:
    goal = s.goal_display if s.goal_display is not None else s.goal_value
    cur = s.current_display if s.current_display is not None else s.current_value
    unit = s.display_unit or ''
    g = f'{goal} {unit}'.strip() if goal is not None else '—'
    c = f'{cur} {unit}'.strip() if cur is not None else '—'
    typ = 'Fixed/Primary' if s.is_active else ('Estimate' if s.use_as_estimate else '—')

print()
print('FIXED+ACTIVE (solver handles — changing these changes the case):')
for s in state.specs:
    if s.is_active:
        goal = s.goal_display if s.goal_display is not None else s.goal_value

print()

for s in state.specs:

h.disconnect()

You’re right to be confused — **CDU is not simple distillation**, so they did **not** fix reflux the way a binary column usually does.

### Simple column (what you’re used to)
Typical Active set (DOF = 0):

- **Reflux ratio** (or reflux flow) → separation / purity  
- **One product rate** (overhead or bottoms) → split / yield  

Reflux is often the main “knob.”

---

### This CDU (T-100) — what they actually fixed

They closed DOF with **product draws + pumparounds + a few overhead/side duties**, and left **reflux as Estimate only**.

Think in groups:

#### 1. Product yields (the “how much of each cut”)
These replace the simple “Ovhd or Btms rate” idea — but for **many products**:

| Fixed Active | Meaning |
|--------------|---------|
| **Naphtha Prod Rate** | How much overhead liquid (naphtha) |
| **Kero_SS Prod Flow** | How much kerosene |
| **Diesel_SS Prod Flow** | How much diesel |
| **AGO_SS Prod Flow** | How much AGO |
| **Vap Prod Flow = 0** | Essentially no offgas draw |
| **Liquid Flow** | Extra overhead liquid handle (with naphtha) |

So the **slate** (naphtha / kero / diesel / AGO amounts) is largely **specified directly**, not “RR will invent the yields.”

Residue is whatever is left after those draws (material balance) — not an Active “bottoms rate” on this list.

#### 2. Internal heat removal (pumparounds) — CDU’s real energy structure
Simple columns often have condenser + reboiler.  
Atmospheric crude towers remove a lot of heat **mid-column** via PAs:

| Fixed Active | Meaning |
|--------------|---------|
| **PA_1 / PA_2 / PA_3 Rate** | Circulation flow of each PA |
| **PA_1 / PA_2 / PA_3 Duty** | Heat removed by each PA |

That sets the **temperature / traffic profile** that makes the cuts possible. On a CDU, this is often more important than top RR.

#### 3. Side-stripper energy
| Fixed Active | Meaning |
|--------------|---------|
| **Kero Reb Duty** | Heat to the kerosene side stripper |

Helps strip lights out of kero (quality), separate from main-column RR.

#### 4. What they did *not* fix as Active
| Spec | Role here |
|------|-----------|
| **Reflux Ratio = 1 (Estimate)** | Monitor / starting guess only. Solver is **not** forced to meet RR = 1. Actual RR came out ≈ **0.71**. |

That is the big mental shift:

> On this case, **RR is not the controlling specification**.  
> **Draws + PA heat** are.

---

### One sentence summary

**They fixed the product rates and the pumparound (and kero reb) energy picture so the tower would produce a defined naphtha/kero/diesel/AGO slate with a physical heat balance — and let reflux float as a calculated/estimate result.**

That is normal atmospheric CDU modeling philosophy; it is why Assist prefers nudging **Diesel_SS Prod Flow** or **PA duty**, not Activating reflux first.

**One answer (plant / HYSYS CDU peer view):**

### Optimize this: **atmospheric furnace duty ↔ overflash (flash-zone vaporization)**

Not reflux. Not “fix every draw.” Not PA rate+duty as the main story.

---

### Why that one (ordinary refinery CDU)

On a real atmospheric unit, the **crude heater / overflash** is the single lever that most governs the whole tower:

1. **How much goes into fractionation vs residue** — flash-zone vaporization sets the ceiling for naphtha/kero/diesel make  
2. **Biggest fired-energy block** on the CDU train (with preheat) — fuel cost + heater reliability / coking  
3. **Quality of every cut** — too little overflash → poor wash, dirty diesel/salt issues, weak separation; too much → you burn fuel for vapor you mostly condense again in PAs  
4. **PAs become meaningful after this** — PA heat recovery to the preheat train only pays if furnace/overflash is posed right

$env:PYTHONIOENCODING='utf-8'; .\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -c @"
from column_api import ColumnController
from hysys_api import HysysController

FRAC = 0.03  # 3% reduction in |PA_1 duty| (energy probe)
SPEC = 'PA_1_Duty(Pa)'

h = HysysController()
h.connect(None)
cols = ColumnController(h)
name = cols.list_columns()[0]
before = cols.inspect(name)

def find(state, n):
    for s in state.specs:
        if s.name == n:
            return s
    return None

pa = find(before, SPEC)
assert pa and pa.is_active and pa.goal_value is not None
old = float(pa.goal_value)
# duty is negative: reduce |duty| => multiply by (1 - FRAC) toward zero
new = old * (1.0 - FRAC)
print('=== PA_1 ENERGY TRIAL (tower-only) ===')
print(f'Column={name}  DOF={before.degrees_of_freedom} converged={before.appears_converged}')
print(f'{SPEC}: goal {old:.6g} -> {new:.6g}  ({FRAC*100:.0f}% less |duty|)')
disp_old = pa.goal_display
print(f'  display was ~{disp_old} {pa.display_unit}')

snap = cols.snapshot(name)
cols.set_spec_goal(name, SPEC, new)
cols.run_column(name)
after = cols.inspect(name)
pa2 = find(after, SPEC)

print()
print('AFTER')
print(f'  DOF={after.degrees_of_freedom} converged={after.appears_converged} physical={after.physical_solution}')
print(f'  Monitor iter={getattr(after, \"monitor_iteration\", \"?\")}')
# try read iter from profile/monitor if present
for s in after.specs:
    if s.is_active and ('Prod' in s.name or 'Naphtha' in s.name or 'Liquid' in s.name):
        g = s.goal_display if s.goal_display is not None else s.goal_value
        c = s.current_display if s.current_display is not None else s.current_value
        print(f'  {s.name}: goal={g} cur={c} err={s.error}')
print(f'  CondQ={after.condenser_duty}')
print(f'  {SPEC} goal_display={pa2.goal_display if pa2 else None} current={pa2.current_display if pa2 else None} err={pa2.error if pa2 else None}')

ok = (
    after.degrees_of_freedom == 0
    and after.appears_converged
    and after.physical_solution
    and after.max_active_spec_error < 5e-3
)
if ok:
    print()
    print('KEEP — still DOF=0, converged, physical (3% PA_1 |duty| cut held).')
    print('Case left with reduced PA_1 duty. Say if you want reverse.')
else:
    print()
    print('REVERSE — health gate failed; restoring snapshot.')
    cols.restore(snap)
    cols.run_column(name)
    restored = cols.inspect(name)
    print(f'Restored: converged={restored.appears_converged} DOF={restored.degrees_of_freedom}')

h.disconnect()
"@

Actually, I'm not sure if we are actually optimizing since there isn't effect to the To the condenser. I'm sorry, do we have a reboiler here? I see a boiler there, I didn't consider it. The reward is for the The bottom part, right? And then Yeah, this is discussion.

Good catch — and you’re right to question whether that was real **optimization**.

### What actually happened
When we cut **PA_1 |duty|**, **condenser duty went up**.

So we mostly **moved heat** from the top PA to the condenser — not clearly “saved energy” on the tower.  
A real tower energy check is closer to:

> **Total heat rejected ≈ Σ|PA duties| + |Condenser duty| (+ side-strip reb if any)**

If PA↓ and Cond↑ by a similar amount, that step is **redistribution**, not a win yet.

---

### Do we have a main reboiler?

**On this atmospheric main tower (T-100): effectively no.**

- Live Assist showed **RebQ = None** for the main column  
- Heat into an atmospheric crude tower usually comes from the **hot feed / flash zone** (furnace upstream — outside our tower-only fence)  
- Residue leaves the **bottom**; you typically do **not** reboil the main bottoms like a binary column

So: **no classic “bottoms reboiler” optimization knob on the main CDU tower.**

---

### What *is* that “boiler” you see?

Almost certainly the **kerosene side-stripper reboiler**:

| Object | Role |
|------|------|
| **Kero Reb Duty** (~7.5e6 Btu/hr, Active) | Reboiler on **Kero_SS** — bottom of the **side stripper**, not main column bottoms |
| Purpose | Strip light ends out of kerosene (quality), small vs PA_1 (~55e6) |

So yes: that reboiler is for the **side-stripper bottom**, not the atmospheric column residue boot.

---

### How this changes the energy discussion

| Heat sink | On T-100 | Comment |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| PA_1 / 2 / 3 | Yes, large | Internal heat removal |
| Condenser | Yes | Overhead heat removal |
| Main reboiler | **No** | Don’t hunt for one |
| Kero reb | Yes, smaller | Side stripper only |

**Tower-only “energy optimize”** should watch **PA + condenser (and maybe kero reb)** together — not PA_1 alone.

---

### Discussion takeaway

1. Primary flows (naphtha + kero) — still valid  
2. PA_1-first — still the biggest **PA** knob  
3. But we should redefine success as: **lower total tower heat rejection (PA+Cond)** while holding naphtha/kero rates — not “lower PA_1 even if Cond climbs”

If you agree, next discussion step is only: pick a **total heat metric** (Σ|PA| + |Cond|) before any more GoalValue moves.
